methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Vyriy Drone is a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Oleksiy Babenko. The target operates within a Defense & Security sector that includes 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, among them Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with aligned valuation tiers to support a reliable median-based analysis.

The valuation relies on stage-progression methodology applied to the company's $4M+ total capital raised as a proxy baseline. Using a standard Growth-stage step-up multiplier of 1.2x yields an implied valuation of $5M. This approach is grounded in observed progression patterns but lacks triangulation from peer multiples due to sparse comparable data at the precise stage and scale of this target.

The analysis supports a range of $4M to $6M, with $5M as the central estimate. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological input rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent transaction multiples in the narrow peer universe, unspecified current-stage revenue or unit economics, and the company's exact positioning relative to the broader defense technology landscape. Further data on revenue traction, customer concentration, and comparable M&A activity in early-stage defense tech would sharpen this valuation materially.
